description: Shared Slack channel between Acme Health and DataBridge Systems for Project Meridian coordination. CloudPeak does not have access.

messages.author: t.reyes
messages.text: Sarah, Marcus — channel is set up. This is the best place for day-to-day coordination between our teams. @k.park will be watching this too for scheduling / account items

messages.author: sarah.chen
messages.text: perfect, thanks Tom. I'll make sure Linda is added too

messages.author: k.park
messages.text: 👋 Kevin here — in the loop!

messages.author: t.reyes
messages.text: heads up — started the field inventory for the mapping. aisha and I are going through the HL7 message samples you sent over. a few questions coming your way later this week

messages.author: marcus.webb
messages.text: great, we're available. linda is the go-to for HL7 segment questions

messages.author: t.reyes
messages.text: @linda.marsh — quick question: in the OBX segment for lab results, are you sometimes sending OBX-5 (observation value) as a CE type (coded element) even when the LOINC code is in OBX-3? or is OBX-3 always the code?

messages.author: linda.marsh
messages.text: OBX-3 is always the LOINC code. OBX-5 type depends on the test — quantitative results are NM type, qualitative are ST. you'll occasionally see CE in OBX-5 for microbiology sensitivities but that should always have OBX-3 populated too

messages.author: t.reyes
messages.text: perfect, that's clear. one more: for the result_comment_routing_flag (your custom extension #7) — is there a defined set of values or is it free-text?

messages.author: linda.marsh
messages.text: defined set. values are: STAT, CRITICAL, ROUTE_LAB, ROUTE_PHYSICIAN, ROUTE_BILLING. sometimes multiple flags on one result — e.g., CRITICAL and ROUTE_PHYSICIAN together. that's why the cardinality needs to be 0..*

messages.author: t.reyes
messages.text: noted on cardinality, I'll fix that in the mapping doc. thanks Linda, this is helpful

messages.author: t.reyes
messages.text: mapping v1 just went out via email. let me know when you've had a chance to look it over

messages.author: marcus.webb
messages.text: got it, thanks

messages.author: t.reyes
messages.text: @marcus.webb @sarah.chen — quick check-in ahead of the Jan 22 review deadline. Let us know if you have any questions while you're going through the mapping doc. Aisha and I have been working on the transformation logic this week but happy to jump on a quick call if anything in the document is unclear.

messages.author: marcus.webb
messages.text: Tom, Kevin — just sent the v1.1 review comments via email. significant items in ADT section and on the custom extensions. please confirm receipt

messages.author: a.patel
messages.text: @marcus.webb — saw the review comments came through yesterday. Tom has them and should be responding shortly. I also found a potential edge case in the ADT A03 mapping that may overlap with your section 3 comments — will flag it when we send back the revised doc.

messages.author: sarah.chen
messages.text: Tom / Kevin — just following up on Marcus's message Monday. Have you had a chance to look at the review comments?

messages.author: marcus.webb
messages.text: @a.patel — Aisha, any update on the review response? We sent our comments to Tom on Monday and haven't heard anything back. Getting concerned given the timeline. Can you let us know where things stand?

messages.author: sarah.chen
messages.text: Kevin, Tom — I sent a calendar invite for a sync, haven't heard back. Can someone confirm availability?

messages.author: sarah.chen
messages.text: Team — second calendar invite sent. Please respond. We need to sync before end of week.

messages.author: a.patel
messages.text: Hi everyone — Aisha here. I've taken over as technical lead from Tom (you may have seen Sarah's email). I've reviewed the Jan 20th comments and I'm working through them. I'll have v1.2 to Marcus by Monday Feb 2. sorry for the radio silence from our side — it wasn't intentional and I know it put you in a bad spot

messages.author: marcus.webb
messages.text: Thanks Aisha. We appreciate the transparency. Feb 2 for v1.2 works. Let us know if you have questions on any of the comments

messages.author: linda.marsh
messages.text: Aisha — I also sent Tom an email on Jan 23 about extension #12 with more context on the reference lab codes. you should have that in his inbox if you have access to it. happy to resend if easier

messages.author: a.patel
messages.text: yes I have access to that inbox, I'll find it. thank you

messages.author: a.patel
messages.text: @marcus.webb @linda.marsh — v1.2 is in your inboxes. I believe I've addressed all the comments. The external_lab_source_id is now a Coding type using the code system Linda provided. please let me know if anything looks off

messages.author: linda.marsh
messages.text: reviewing now 👀

messages.author: marcus.webb
messages.text: Aisha — looks good overall. One open item: the A03 discharge disposition mapping (rows 88-94) — I see it's there now but the value set reference is pointing to an older version of the NUBC code list. Linda, can you confirm which version we use?

messages.author: linda.marsh
messages.text: we use NUBC v2023. the one in v1.2 is pointing to v2019. Aisha can you update that reference?

messages.author: a.patel
messages.text: done — updating now. will have v1.3 to you within the hour

messages.author: a.patel
messages.text: v1.3 in your inboxes. architecture package also sent to Priya and Jordan at CloudPeak

messages.author: marcus.webb
messages.text: ✅ Approved. Marking data mapping v1.3 as the approved baseline. Thank you Aisha, this was good work under pressure.

messages.author: sarah.chen
messages.text: 🎉 great work everyone. Aisha please tell us your availability for the integration testing cycle — we need to coordinate with CloudPeak on the environment readiness schedule
